title: Art Review: ‘25 Years of Hamburger Eyes’ at SFPL | KQED
content: ## The Bay. Bay Area-raised host Ericka Cruz Guevarra brings you context and analysis to make sense of the news. Bay Area-raised host Ericka Cruz Guevarra brings you context and analysis to make sense of the news. Support KQED by using your donor-advised fund to make a charitable gift. # Celebrate the Grit and Glory of ‘25 Years of Hamburger Eyes’ at the SF Public Library. He is holding a bunch of flowers and standing in front of a wall full of framed black and white photography. At the forefront was *Hamburger Eyes*, a fanzine that captured and celebrated the everyday realities of city living in black-and-white candids. For the next five months, the San Francisco Public Library’s Jewett Gallery is exhibiting work by 83 photographers who have featured in the pages of *Hamburger Eyes*. *‘The Continuing Story Of Life On Earth: 25 Years of Hamburger Eyes’ is on view at the San Francisco Public Library’s Jewett Gallery through Sept.
